rulings.Calendar Matter: Plant Insulation'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 14, Plant Insulation's Motion For Summary Judgment is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ARLEY J SMITH III et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: NATALIE BUFORD et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT MARC PAUL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. IN A WRONGFUL DEATH ACTION, STANDARD INTERROGATORIES COUPLED WITH THE HEIRS TESTIMONY IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ARE NECESSARY TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFFS LACK EVIDENCE TO PROVE THEIR CASE. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY SNODGRASS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OFF IS CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY SNODGRASS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANT VACCO INDUSTRIES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE BY ESTABLISHING THE FACTS FOR THE GOVERNMENT CONTRACTOR DEFENSE. THE KESKINEN DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE FOR A PROPER FOUNDATION HAS BEEN PROVIDED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO CONTINUE IS DENIED FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT EXPLAINED THE REASON FOR THE DELAY IN SEEKING THE TESTIMONY OR WHAT FACTS PLAINTIFF EXPECTS TO DISCOVER. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY W ELWOOD VS. INFRASTRUCTURE TECHNOLOGY SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 8, DEFENDANTS INFRASTRUCTURE TECHNOLOGY SERVICES, INC. AND STEPHEN COOK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE IN THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE "CLIENT" REFERRED TO BY PLAINTIFF IN HIS DEPOSITION TESTIMONY WAS NOT DEFENDANT. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO FACT 6 AS HEARSAY IS SUSTAINED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING ISSUES FOR A RULING ON EITHER WOULD NOT RESOLVE A CAUSE OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: COURTENEY EVERSOLE VS. DOUGLAS E PHANCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue (Wrong Venue);
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 9, DEFENDANT SHERI ROSSILLO'S Motion To Change Venue (Wrong Venue) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT DOES NOT PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE AS TO ROSSILLO'S RESIDENCE. COUNSEL'S HEARSAY DECLARATION IS INSUFFICIENT. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT OTHER DEFENDANTS DO NOT RESIDE IN SAN FRANCISCO. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT J. BOCKELMAN VS. JONATHAN NEWHOUSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment Of The Pleadings And Notice Of Request For Judicial Notice; P/A; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Deft'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 10, DEFENDANT JONATHAN NEWHOUSE'S Motion For Judgment Of The Pleadings IS OFF CALENDAR, MOTION UNTIMELY PER CCP 1005. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W THAYER VS. CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 11, DEFENDANT CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON A WRITING. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE BEING NO OPPOSITION. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED ANYTHING MORE THAN A PRIVATE DISPUTE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED A FALSE ADVERTISING CLAIM. 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION; LEAVE TO AMEND IS DENIED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION; HEARING IS REQUIRED IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS LEAVE TO AMEND THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: GLENRIDGE APARTMENTS RESIDENTS COUNCIL, INC. VS. MALA TAYLOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmotion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 14, 2008, line 12, PLAINTIFF GLENRIDGE APARTMENTS RESIDENTS COUNCIL, INC.'S Motion To Enforce Stipulated Settlement IS DENIED. DEFENDANT, MALA TAYLOR, IS DECEASED, AND PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING TO ENFORCE A STIPULATION ENTERED INTO WITH TAYLOR. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROCEED WITH THIS CLAIM UNTIL DEFENDANT'S PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE IS SUBSTITUTED INTO THE CASE AS A DEFENDANT. CCP SECTION 377.41. IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A NEW ACTION AGAINST THE CURRENT PERSONS RESIDING ON THE PREMISES. =(302/PJM/PB)
